title: [InstCombine] Fold an unsigned icmp of ucmp/scmp with a constant to an icmp of the original arguments

tests.outside_scope_reason: Summary mentions 'constant > 1' generally but doesn't specifically cover the boundary case of constant = 2, which is the minimum value that triggers the optimization

alive2.log:
----------------------------------------
define i1 @scmp_ult_const_exactly_2(i32 %x, i32 %y) {
#0:
  %#1 = scmp i8 i32 %x, %y
  %#range_0_%#1 = !range i8 %#1, i8 255, i8 2
  %#2 = icmp ult i8 %#range_0_%#1, 2
  ret i1 %#2
}
=>
define i1 @scmp_ult_const_exactly_2(i32 %x, i32 %y) {
#0:
  %#1 = icmp sge i32 %x, %y
  ret i1 %#1
}
Transformation seems to be correct!

Summary:
  1 correct transformations
  0 incorrect transformations
  0 failed-to-prove transformations
  0 Alive2 errors

tests.outside_scope_reason: Summary states 'constant not 0 or -1' but doesn't explicitly mention constant = 1 as a specific test case for ucmp where the intrinsic range is [0,1]

alive2.log:
----------------------------------------
define i1 @ucmp_ugt_const_exactly_1(i32 %x, i32 %y) {
#0:
  %#1 = ucmp i8 i32 %x, %y
  %#range_0_%#1 = !range i8 %#1, i8 255, i8 2
  %#2 = icmp ugt i8 %#range_0_%#1, 1
  ret i1 %#2
}
=>
define i1 @ucmp_ugt_const_exactly_1(i32 %x, i32 %y) {
#0:
  %#1 = icmp ult i32 %x, %y
  ret i1 %#1
}
Transformation seems to be correct!

Summary:

  0 incorrect transformations

  0 Alive2 errors

tests.outside_scope_reason: Summary doesn't mention different integer widths (i64 vs i32) for the cmp intrinsic operands, only discusses the comparison result type

alive2.log:
----------------------------------------
define i1 @scmp_ult_i64_wide_type(i64 %x, i64 %y) {
#0:
  %#1 = scmp i8 i64 %x, %y
  %#range_0_%#1 = !range i8 %#1, i8 255, i8 2
  %#2 = icmp ult i8 %#range_0_%#1, 5
  ret i1 %#2
}
=>
define i1 @scmp_ult_i64_wide_type(i64 %x, i64 %y) {
#0:
  %#1 = icmp sge i64 %x, %y
  ret i1 %#1
}
Transformation seems to be correct!
